questions.questionText: The first-line diagnostic test for HIV screening in most settings is:
questions.explanation: Fourth-generation assays detect p24 antigen and antibodies, enabling earlier diagnosis.

questions.questionText: In HIV management, Pneumocystis jirovecii prophylaxis is typically initiated when CD4 count falls below:
questions.explanation: TMP-SMX prophylaxis is recommended when CD4 is below 200 cells per microliter.

questions.questionText: The most common etiology of community-acquired pneumonia in adults is:
questions.explanation: Streptococcus pneumoniae remains a leading cause of CAP.

questions.questionText: A patient with severe sepsis and hypotension despite adequate fluids is in:
questions.explanation: Septic shock is defined by persistent hypotension requiring vasopressors after fluid resuscitation.

questions.questionText: The most appropriate initial vasopressor in septic shock is:
questions.explanation: Norepinephrine is recommended first-line in septic shock.

questions.questionText: In enteric fever, the antibiotic resistance concern has increased particularly for:
questions.explanation: Drug-resistant Salmonella typhi strains are a major therapeutic concern.

questions.questionText: The gold standard for diagnosis of infective endocarditis microbiologically is:
questions.explanation: Multiple blood cultures before antibiotics are critical to identify causative organisms.

questions.questionText: A ring-enhancing brain lesion in HIV patient with CD4 below 100 most commonly suggests:
questions.explanation: CNS toxoplasmosis is a common opportunistic infection with ring-enhancing lesions in advanced HIV.

questions.questionText: In suspected bacterial meningitis, empiric antibiotics should be started:
questions.optionB: Immediately after blood cultures, without unnecessary delay
questions.explanation: Early empiric antibiotics significantly improve outcomes in bacterial meningitis.

questions.questionText: The first-line treatment of uncomplicated falciparum malaria in many regions is based on:
questions.explanation: ACT is recommended for uncomplicated falciparum malaria due to resistance patterns.

questions.questionText: A patient on broad-spectrum antibiotics develops watery diarrhea and pseudomembranes on colonoscopy. Most likely organism is:
questions.explanation: Antibiotic-associated colitis with pseudomembranes is classic for C. difficile infection.

questions.questionText: The key principle in preventing antimicrobial resistance is:
questions.optionA: Use broad-spectrum antibiotics for all fever
questions.optionB: Antibiotic stewardship with targeted therapy and de-escalation
questions.optionD: Stop treatment on day 1 once afebrile
questions.explanation: Rational antibiotic selection, culture-guided therapy, and de-escalation are core stewardship steps.
